title: viola aethnensis subsp. calabra
intro: Viola aethnensis subsp. calabra, also known as the Calabrian violet, is a subspecies of the mountain pansy plant, native to the Calabria region in Italy. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Viola aethnensis subsp. calabra are attributed to its flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Viola aethnensis subsp. calabra can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
